threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still take the bus when the Tube's down. Locals just walk or cycle, strike chaos is a way of life here.
Heard a guy on the 196 last night talking about his cats like they were his kids. Serious debate over who gets the best spot on the sofa.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a couple arguing passionately in Turkish over the price of fresh produce at the market, I realized how alive this place really is.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Neukölln do you find a cozy café amidst an old bingo hall, where locals sip coffee while chatting about last night's match. Total vibe!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only a true local knows to dodge the BART delays and still be ten minutes early to a taco spot. Tourists wear watches; we check train apps.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 38 Geary crammed with tourists, but the driver preaches about local gems like it's a guided tour. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists assume every bar serves tapas at any hour, but many local spots only do it before 9 PM. After that, it’s drinks only.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their struggle with the tram system; they always seem surprised when it comes to a sudden halt, like it’s a game.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused when the bus driver doesn't wait for you to sit down before taking off. Classic newbie move.
Caught the 59 from Brixton to Croydon last night; the driver was blasting 90s R&B. Seriously, the vibe was better than a club!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw a guy casually duct-taping his bike back together at the corner bakery while ordering a croissant.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find guys selling vinyl records out of a bicycle cart, stopping traffic just to spin some tunes. Pure Berlin vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here when they can name the last time the BART had a full week without a delay. That’s a rare breed now.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy playing a saxophone on the Muni bus—totally off key but somehow perfect for the chaos. Only in the Tenderloin.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia's plazas are for lounging. In reality, they're often packed for local festivals, and they miss the real vibe when they just pass through.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always spot them by their hesitant pause at crosswalks—still learning that the green man doesn’t always mean go in this city.
